# Story 1.1: Capture Manual Source Records

Status: in-progress

<!-- Generated by bmad-create-story on 2026-07-08. Tenant writes remain gated. -->

## Story

As Doug,
I want to manually capture a work-context source record,
so that non-email commitments and decisions can enter the Council without pretending they came from Outlook.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given the Council MVP is running without live Microsoft source access, when Doug creates a manual Source Record, then the record must capture source system, source kind, native object reference or manual reference, capture time, capture actor, data boundary policy, and source-to-work-item rationale, and no Work Item is created until an explicit extraction step runs.
2. Given source content is sensitive or source body policy is unknown, when a manual Source Record is saved, then the system must support link-only, hash-only, or summary-only capture, and the source body policy must be visible on the Source Record.

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Confirm story gates before implementation.
  - [x] Verify tenant-write approval, publisher prefix, source body policy, and model-driven app acceptance before any live Dataverse write.
  - [x] If approval is not present, keep work local to manifest, validation, and documentation artifacts and do not mark the story done.
- [x] Implement the manual Source Record slice in the Dataverse MVP artifacts.
  - [x] Use `Council Source Record` / `com_councilsourcerecord` as the record type; do not introduce a generic item table.
  - [x] Preserve required fields from `source-record-contract.md`: stable Council source ID, source system, source kind, source object reference, captured time, captured by, data boundary policy, extraction status, and source-to-work-item rationale.
  - [x] Ensure manual capture can use `source_system=manual`, `source_kind=manual_note`, and a human-entered `source_object_ref` without Outlook or Graph access.
  - [x] Ensure data boundary choices include `link_only`, `hash_only`, `summary_allowed`, `full_snapshot_allowed`, and `unknown`.
- [x] Preserve the Source Record / Work Item boundary.
  - [x] Saving a Source Record must not create or mutate a Work Item.
  - [x] Work Item creation remains an explicit extraction step owned by later stories.
  - [x] If any sample data is used, create Source Record evidence only unless Doug explicitly approves a vertical-slice sample that includes Work Items and Receipts.
- [ ] Configure the first review surface after tenant approval.
  - [ ] In the model-driven `Council Queue` app, expose Source Records in the Intake group.
  - [ ] Provide New Source Records and Held Source Records views.
  - [ ] Put source reference, data boundary policy, sensitivity/retention notes, extraction status, and rationale in the visible form path.
- [x] Strengthen validation around this slice.
  - [x] Keep `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/dataverse-deployment-plan.ps1` dry-run unless the reviewed write implementation and Doug approval exist.
  - [x] Update local validation only if new artifacts are added.
  - [x] Run the required local validation commands before marking implementation ready for review.

## Dev Notes

### Product and Architecture Guardrails

- BMAD is the planning and implementation harness, not the product runtime. Do not model BMAD as a Council feature. [Source: `_bmad-output/project-context.md#Technology-Stack-&-Versions`]
- The product is work-item-first, but every intake event starts as a Source Record. Source Records never become Work Items by mutation. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture/architecture-The-Council-of-Minions-2026-07-06/source-record-contract.md#Invariant`]
- MVP intake is Outlook-first plus manual capture, and the first review surface is Minion Brief plus Council Queue. This story covers the manual capture side only. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prds/prd-The-Council-of-Minions-2026-07-06/prd.md#6-MVP-Scope-Direction`]
- Source projects are conceptual inputs only. Do not copy Open Brain, Open Engine, Linear, Slack, Supabase, or non-Microsoft operating surfaces into the implementation. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prds/prd-The-Council-of-Minions-2026-07-06/addendum.md#Interpretation-Boundary`]
- The Council Semantic Contract owns canonical terms. Dataverse metadata, Dataverse semantic model, Fabric IQ, Fabric Graph, Copilot Studio, and future agent knowledge are projections or bindings, not competing sources of truth. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture/architecture-The-Council-of-Minions-2026-07-06/semantic-contract.md#Authority`]
- Live Microsoft tenant behavior, data writes, published agents, app registrations, automations, or external actions remain `VERIFY IN TENANT` until evidence exists. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture/architecture-The-Council-of-Minions-2026-07-06/ARCHITECTURE-SPINE.md#AD-10---Tenant-interaction-remains-VERIFY-IN-TENANT-ADOPTED`]

### Runtime Decision

- The practical MVP runtime is Dataverse operational store plus a model-driven app named `Council Queue`, pending Doug approval and tenant validation. [Source: `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/runtime-setup-baseline-2026-07-08.md#Runtime-Decision`]
- There is no custom web app, local dev server, published Copilot Studio agent, Power Automate flow, app registration, Fabric dependency, or Outlook/Graph live-read dependency for this first manual slice. [Source: `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/runtime-setup-baseline-2026-07-08.md#Non-Runtime-Boundaries`]
- The repo currently represents the Dataverse target through manifest and PowerShell planning/validation artifacts. After tenant write approval, committed implementation evidence must include an exported unmanaged solution, unpacked solution source, or a reviewed equivalent ALM artifact. Screenshots alone are not sufficient. [Source: `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/runtime-setup-baseline-2026-07-08.md#Packaging-Boundary`]

### Dataverse Slice

The existing manifest already contains the Source Record table shape. Build on it instead of creating a parallel model.

Relevant manifest entries:

- Target environment URL: `https://sdhdev.crm.dynamics.com`
- Environment ID: `ba9a96b2-f562-40f6-931d-6b55873954ee`
- Organization ID: `0c0fa4db-8614-ef11-9f83-000d3a342d36`
- Solution unique name: `CouncilOfMinionsMVP`
- Placeholder publisher prefix: `com`
- Source Record table: `com_councilsourcerecord`
- Model-driven app: `Council Queue` / `com_CouncilQueue`
- Default source body policy guard: `link_only`

Required Source Record columns for this story:

| Column | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `com_council_source_record_id` | Stable Council source identity, distinct from Dataverse row ID. |
| `com_source_system` | For manual records, use `manual`. |
| `com_source_kind` | For manual notes, use `manual_note`. |
| `com_source_object_ref` | Native source ID or manual reference. Required even when no URL exists. |
| `com_source_object_url` | Optional; allowed only when a resolvable link is safe. |
| `com_captured_at` | Capture timestamp. Use ISO 8601 semantics. |
| `com_captured_by` | Human, Minion, connector, or trusted-source rule that captured it. |
| `com_content_snapshot_ref` | Optional; use only when data boundary policy permits summary/full snapshot. |
| `com_content_hash` | Optional; supports hash-only capture. |
| `com_permission_snapshot` | Best-known access scope; may be `unknown` in MVP. |
| `com_sensitivity_label` | Sensitivity/classification when visible. |
| `com_retention_or_hold_flags` | Retention/legal hold/policy flags when visible. |
| `com_extraction_status` | Initial manual capture should start as `new` or `held`, not extracted. |
| `com_source_to_work_item_rationale` | Why work may or may not be proposed later. |
| `com_data_boundary_policy` | Must be visible on the record and drive allowed body capture behavior. |

[Source: `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/dataverse-mvp-schema-manifest.json`; `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/dataverse-mvp-schema-plan-2026-07-08.md#1-Council-Source-Record`]

### UX Requirements

- The first user experience is a model-driven business app, not a custom dashboard or inbox-only assistant. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/ux-designs/ux-The-Council-of-Minions-2026-07-08/EXPERIENCE.md#Foundation`]
- Source Records live under the Intake area. Default Source Record grids should show source system, source kind, extraction status, data boundary policy, source object reference, captured by, and drift indicator. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/ux-designs/ux-The-Council-of-Minions-2026-07-08/EXPERIENCE.md#Component-Patterns`]
- The Source Record form should make source link/object reference, data boundary policy, sensitivity, retention/hold flags, extraction status, rationale, and related Work Items visible before less-used metadata. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/ux-designs/ux-The-Council-of-Minions-2026-07-08/EXPERIENCE.md#Component-Patterns`]
- If source body policy is unknown, extraction commands require an explicit safe fallback such as link-only or hash-only. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/ux-designs/ux-The-Council-of-Minions-2026-07-08/EXPERIENCE.md#Key-Flows`]
- Use Microsoft model-driven app defaults where possible; avoid a decorative custom dashboard. [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/ux-designs/ux-The-Council-of-Minions-2026-07-08/DESIGN.md#Brand-&-Style`]

### Files Being Modified or Extended

Read these files completely before implementation changes:

| Path | Current state | Preserve |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/dataverse-mvp-schema-manifest.json` | Defines all MVP choices, tables, guards, target env metadata, and `Council Queue` navigation. | Do not collapse tables. Keep `requiresDougApprovalBeforeWrite=true` and guard flags unless Doug explicitly changes them. |
| `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/dataverse-manifest-validate.ps1` | Validates manifest structure, choices, table references, lookup targets, contracts, and model-driven navigation table refs. | Keep validation deterministic and local. Do not require tenant auth. |
| `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/dataverse-deployment-plan.ps1` | Emits dry-run deployment plan and intentionally throws if `-ExecuteWrites` is used. | Do not implement live writes unless write approval and reviewed write implementation exist. |
| `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/dataverse-preflight-readonly.ps1` | Runs `pac auth who`, `pac env who`, and `pac env list-settings`; appends evidence and verifies environment/org IDs. | Keep it read-only. Do not add metadata writes. |
| `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/council-mvp-local-validate.ps1` | Validates local packet, manifest, dry-run behavior, sprint status, UX spines, and stale gates. | Extend only for new source-controlled artifacts. Keep success marker `COUNCIL_MVP_LOCAL_VALIDATE_OK`. |
| `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/tenant-validation-evidence-2026-07-08.md` | Evidence target for read-only tenant validation. | Append evidence only from read-only validation until writes are approved. |

If tenant writes are approved, also produce source-controlled ALM evidence for the actual Dataverse solution components. Do not rely on portal-only state.

### Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- Do not create a Work Item automatically when a manual Source Record is saved.
- Do not use Outlook IDs or Dataverse row IDs as Council product identities.
- Do not store sensitive body text when the source body policy is `unknown`, `link_only`, or `hash_only`.
- Do not treat a model-driven view or Brief as the source of truth for queue state.
- Do not add Power Automate, Copilot Studio, Fabric, app registration, or Outlook/Graph scope as a hidden dependency for this story.
- Do not mark the story done from a dry-run script alone if the intended acceptance path is live model-driven capture.

## Testing Requirements

Minimum local validation before review:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File _bmad-output\implementation-artifacts\dataverse-manifest-validate.ps1
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File _bmad-output\implementation-artifacts\dataverse-deployment-plan.ps1
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File _bmad-output\implementation-artifacts\council-mvp-local-validate.ps1
git diff --check
```

Expected local success marker:

```text
COUNCIL_MVP_LOCAL_VALIDATE_OK
```

Tenant validation after Doug approval and interactive auth:

```powershell
pac auth create --environment ba9a96b2-f562-40f6-931d-6b55873954ee --name Council-SDH-Dev
pac auth who
pac env who
pac env list-settings
```

Do not run tenant writes until `pac env who` proves the expected environment ID and organization ID, Doug approves writes, and the publisher prefix/source body policy are confirmed.

Manual acceptance tests after approved Dataverse implementation:

1. Create a manual Source Record with `source_system=manual` and `source_kind=manual_note`.
2. Confirm required source identity, capture, actor, data boundary, and rationale fields are visible and saved.
3. Confirm no Work Item is created by saving the Source Record.
4. Confirm `link_only`, `hash_only`, and `summary_allowed` policies can be represented without storing disallowed body text.
5. Confirm Source Records appear in Intake / Source Records and New Source Records views.
6. Confirm held/unknown data boundary records are visible as reviewable records and do not silently extract.

## Definition of Done

- A manual Source Record can be represented in the selected MVP implementation surface.
- Source Record and Work Item identities remain distinct.
- Saving a Source Record does not create a Work Item.
- Data boundary policy is visible and constrains body capture.
- Local validation passes.
- If tenant implementation occurs, tenant evidence and solution/export artifacts are committed.
- No outbound action, flow publish, agent publish, app registration, Fabric mutation, or Outlook/Graph write occurs.

## Open Questions / Required User Decisions

These are saved for the end of story creation and must be closed before live implementation completion:

1. Does Doug approve Dataverse as the MVP operational store for this first slice?
2. Does Doug accept the model-driven app as the first Council Queue / Minion Brief surface?
3. What publisher prefix should replace or confirm placeholder `com`?
4. What source body policy is allowed for sample records: link-only, hash-only, summary allowed, or full snapshot allowed?
5. Is Dataverse sandbox write approval granted for `https://sdhdev.crm.dynamics.com` after read-only preflight matches the expected environment?

## Project Context Reference

Read `_bmad-output/project-context.md` before implementation. The highest-risk rules for this story are:

- Use `work item` as the canonical execution object, but do not create it during source capture.
- Treat emails, chats, notes, meeting outputs, and manual captures as Source Records first.
- Every Work Item later created from this source must preserve provenance and rationale.
- Treat Dataverse semantic/model metadata as a projection of the Council Semantic Contract, not the canonical ontology.
- Mark tenant-specific unknowns as `VERIFY IN TENANT`.
